mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 1.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor demand and income growth, which sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS growth does not correlate positively with subsequent 60-day SPX returns (Spearman rho < 0.15 over rolling 3-year windows), or SPX declines in 50%+ of periods with positive PAYEMS momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1000 thousand units signals weakening demand expectations and reduced construction activity, which compresses near-term GDP growth forecasts and triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1000 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the correlation between HOUST declines and subsequent negative returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) move in opposite directions: when INDPRO declines while CPIAUCSL rises, stagflation expectations emerge, causing equity investors to reduce risk exposure and sell equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth turns negative while CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of stagflation-triggered instances, or the joint signal (negative INDPRO + positive CPIAUCSL) does not outperform single-series signals in predicting negative returns (Rank IC difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and employment (PAYEMS) both accelerate simultaneously, signaling strong consumer income and spending momentum that drives corporate earnings revisions upward, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as the market reprices earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.2% in the same month
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand and labor strength
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of co-accelerating instances, or the joint signal does not improve prediction accuracy over single-series models (Rank IC improvement < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5%, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which compresses near-term earnings expectations and triggers equity de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors front-run recession concerns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.5% for the first time in a 12-month window
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of UNRATE spike instances, or the lead-lag relationship between UNRATE increases and negative returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 4.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3.5% year-over-year, the real rate is restrictive and growth expectations compress, causing equity investors to reduce exposure and sell, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of restrictive real-rate instances, or the interaction signal does not outperform single-series FEDFUNDS signal (Rank IC difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3.0% annualized, signaling robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as investors increase equity allocations in response to stronger growth.
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth above 3.0% does not correlate positively with subsequent 60-day SPX returns (Spearman rho < 0.12 over rolling 3-year windows), or SPX declines in more than 45% of high-growth quarters.
